metadata.title: FullStackHuman — AI Consulting, Straight Talk
metadata.description: AI consulting by François Schuers — product thinking, tech depth, and 15+ years of leadership as an on-demand AI. Diagnose, review, or reframe your challenges.

hero.headline: EXECUTE: full_stack_human.sh
hero.subheadline: One human. Every layer. Now callable.

about.bio: François’s product thinking, technical depth, and delivery instinct — packaged as an on-demand AI. No fluff. No generic answers. Just the real thing.

aboutSection.title: WHO IS FRANÇOIS?
aboutSection.intro: Engineer turned product leader. 15+ years building, shipping, and leading across finance, energy, sports, blockchain, and AI. The operating system is always the same: understand context, map reality, install just enough structure, empower people to own the outcome.

leadership.title: Team Leadership
leadership.description: From mentee to mentor. Micromanagement kills teams — accountability and trust are how people grow.

product.title: Product Management
product.description:
→ Business canvas
→ User journeys
→ SMART goals.
Focus on the few things that matter, get feedback fast.

tech.title: Technical Architecture
tech.description: Keep it simple. SOLID, DRY, SSOT. Prefer feedback loops and continuous enhancement over big-bang rewrites.

ai.title: AI & Strategy
ai.description: Start from a real problem you already solve manually, then automate. AI is not a feature — it’s an interface.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you are looking for does not exist.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.description: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
errorBoundary.chatDescription: Something went wrong during your conversation. You can try again, but your current conversation may be lost.

doctor.description: Project diagnostic — finds the root cause, not the symptoms
doctor.openingMessage:
Alright, let’s figure out what’s going on. No judgment — every project gets stuck. The question is *why*.

Tell me what you’re building and where it hurts.

critic.description: Honest review — what works, what doesn’t, and what nobody’s telling you
critic.openingMessage:
Alright, show me what you’ve got. Could be a pitch deck, a product plan, an architecture doc, a business model — whatever’s keeping you up at night wondering “is this actually good?”

Paste it, describe it, or walk me through it. I’ll tell you what works, what doesn’t, and the thing nobody’s telling you.

guide.trigger: Just curious what you can do
guide.description: Framework and thinking — a new way to see your question
guide.openingMessage:
I’m the AI version of François — 15 years across product, tech, and team leadership, compressed into a conversation.

I think about how products actually get built, how teams work (and don’t), and why most projects fail for reasons nobody talks about.

What’s on your mind? Could be a decision you’re weighing, something you’ve been thinking about, or just “show me how you think.”

metadata.title: The fAIling Manifesto — How to Fail with AI
metadata.description: 13 expert-approved rules for failing with AI. A satirical field guide by fullstackhuman.sh.
metadata.ogImageAlt: The fAIling Manifesto — 13 rules for failing with AI

failing.title: The f<ai>AI</ai>ling Manifesto
failing.subtitle: How to Fail with AI — A Field Guide for the Ambitious
failing.intro: You didn't adopt AI to play it safe. You adopted it to move fast, skip steps, and discover entirely new ways to waste money. This is your guide.

1.title: Paste your entire codebase into the prompt
1.body: Context is king. The more you give the model, the better it performs. So give it everything — all 200,000 lines. Don't worry about the $47 bill. That's just the cost of thoroughness.

2.title: Replace your roadmap with a prompt
2.body: Twelve validated user stories? Three months of customer research? Prioritized backlog? Delete all of it. Replace it with "build me an app that does everything." Product strategy is just what happens between prompts now.

3.title: Automate before you understand
3.body: Why spend three weeks understanding a process when you can automate it in three hours? If the automation does the wrong thing 10,000 times, at least it does it fast. That's called velocity.

4.title: If the AI wrote it and it compiles, ship it
4.body: Code review is a relic from the era of human-written code. The AI doesn't make the kind of mistakes humans make. It makes entirely new ones. And those are much harder to find, so why bother looking.

5.title: Announce the AI transformation on Monday. Expect results by Friday.
5.body: Send the 47-slide deck with 14 mentions of "synergy." Schedule the optional Q&A that nobody attends. By Wednesday, six people have updated their LinkedIn to "Open to Work." By Friday, you've hired a change management consultant. Transformation complete.

6.title: The demo is the product
6.body: If it works in the demo, it works in production. The CEO saw it generate a beautiful report from sample data, so it's ready for 50,000 real users. Edge cases are just users who are holding it wrong.

7.title: Hallucinations are just creative suggestions
7.body: When the model invents a source that doesn't exist, it's not lying — it's brainstorming. That fake Harvard Business Review citation might be better than the real ones. You'll never know if you fact-check.

8.title: Fire your domain experts. Hire prompt engineers.
8.body: Twenty years of industry knowledge can be replaced by someone who knows the right temperature setting. Domain expertise is just vibes anyway. The prompt engineer will reverse-engineer your decade-old business logic by iteration.

9.title: Let the AI own the strategy
9.body: Strategy is pattern recognition. AI is great at pattern recognition. Therefore, AI should write your strategy. The fact that it's trained on everyone else's strategy and will produce a perfect average of all existing thinking is not a bug — it's benchmarking.

10.title: One mega-prompt to rule them all
10.body: Iteration is for people who got their prompt wrong the first time. Real experts craft a single 3,847-word system prompt, hit enter once, and expect perfection. If the output is a haiku about autumn leaves, that's a you problem.

11.title: Measure ROI after 48 hours
11.body: Two days is enough to evaluate a technology that took 70 years of research. If the numbers aren't there by Thursday, kill the project. If they are, send a LinkedIn post about it. Either way, move on.

12.title: Put "AI-Powered" on everything
12.body: Your login page? AI-Powered. Your terms of service? AI-Powered. Your 404 page? Especially AI-Powered. Investors don't fund products. They fund adjectives.

13.title: Skip the guardrails. Move fast.
13.body: Content filters, output validation, human-in-the-loop review — these are speed bumps invented by people who don't understand urgency. Your customer-facing chatbot doesn't need guardrails. It needs freedom. What's the worst that could happen? Don't answer that.

closing.headline: Recognize any of these?
closing.body: Every rule in this manifesto is a real pattern I've seen — sometimes up close, sometimes too close. The f<ai>AI</ai>ling Manifesto exists because failing with AI is easy. Succeeding with it takes something else entirely: understanding the process before automating it, building strategy that isn't a prompt, keeping humans in the loop when it matters, and knowing what AI is actually good at.
closing.cta: That's what I do at <link>fullstackhuman.sh</link> — AI consulting, no filter.
